item.title: Personal
item.description:
CAVEAT. This is a draft post. The contents shown here shall be developed fully soon.

Books

I regularly update my reading status at Goodreads. Here is the list of my favorite authors. If you ask me the books that influenced me the most so far, they would be The Bible, Plato’s Republic, Augustine’s Confessions, and Frankl’s Man’s Search for Meaning. I also write reviews from time to time. Feel free to follow me!

Films

I regularly update films I’ve watched at Letterboxd. Feel free to follow me! If you ask me my top three favorite directors, they would be Stanley Kubrick, Ingmar Bergman, and Akira Kurosawa. For great TV miniseries, I recommend “Fanny and Alexander” (Bergman, 1983) and “Dekalog” (Kieślowski, 1988). (“Berlin Alexanderplatz” (Fassbinder, 1980) is on my to-watch list.)

Quotes

  • “Zorba,” I shouted, “come, teach me to dance!” Zorba leaped to his feet, his face glowing. “Dance, Boss?” he asked. “Dance? Come!” (Zorba the Greek, Nikos Kazantzakis; tr. Peter Bien) A memorable associated video clip.
  • There is a time for everything, and a season for every activity under heavens: … (Ecclesiastes 3:1-8, NIV). Full verse reference here. There is a song inspired by these verses.
